revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Ena Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Ena shrine takayama.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]] Enako Town 1290 | coordinates={{Coord|36.1351|N|137.270997|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | prefecture={{ill|Takayama|qid=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} }} '''Ena Shrine''' (荏名神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Enako Town, [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} under the name "Ena Shrine" in {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|qid=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|qid=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)|1=Ōno District, Gifu}} and was previously classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}.
{{ill|Enano Shrine|lt=Enano Shrine|qid=Q135039909|1=Enano Shrine}} candidate.<ref>[https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181963 DB]</ref>
== Deities == The shrine's enshrined deities are [[Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi no Mikoto]] and Ena Great deity. According to the "Shinmeichō Kōshō" (神名帳考證), the deity is identified as Ōyatsuhime no Mikoto. Until the medieval period, the shrine was known as "Inaki Mori Koyasu Daimyōjin" and was revered as a deity of safe childbirth. Although the term "Yamashiro no Enatsuhime" appears in the [[Kojiki]]'s account of Emperor Kaika, any connection to this shrine is uncertain. == History == The origins of the shrine are unclear. It is listed as "Hida Province, Ōno District, Ena Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, categorized as a small shrine. However, the shrine fell into decline over time, and its precise location was lost. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka era|1=Bunka}} (1815), the scholar [[Tanaka Taishū]] from Takayama identified the Koyasu Daimyōjin in Enako Town's Inaki Forest as Ena Shrine. This was based on the interpretation of "Ena" as referring to "placenta" (胞衣). The shrine was subsequently renamed Ena Shrine and restored. In September of the 4th year of the Meiji period (1871), it was officially Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Another theory posits that the original Ena Shrine is located at {{ill|Kōjin Shrine (Takayama)|ja|荒神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin Shrine (Takayama)|1=Kō Shrine (Q11618837)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11618837}} in Enako Town 4946. == Cultural Properties == * **Ena Bunko Storehouse** (岐阜県指定史跡) - A library of documents belonging to [[Tanaka Taishū]].<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7334.html |title=Ena Bunko Storehouse |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-05-10}}</ref> * **Sacred Bridge of Ena Shrine** - Designed by Tanaka Taishū and registered as a designated cultural property of Takayama City.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=https://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000848/1000891.html |title=Sacred Bridge of Ena Shrine with Design Plan by Tanaka Taishū |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub}}{{wikidata link|Q11618316}}
{{translated page|ja|荏名神社|version=98262191|comment=Imported full ja history}}
<!--Ena Shrine-->
